macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Wells Fargo Files Trademark for WFUSD Stablecoin
items.summary: Wells Fargo has filed a crypto-related trademark application for WFUSD, signaling a deeper push into cryptocurrency and blockchain services, including payment processing. This move indicates major bank adoption in crypto.

items.title: BlackRock Launches Ethereum Staking ETF
items.summary: BlackRock has officially launched its Ethereum staking ETF, enabling staking of ETH holdings and potentially driving rotation into Ethereum amid market pressures. This is seen as a significant development for institutional ETH adoption.

items.title: Mastercard Launches New Crypto Partner Program
items.summary: Mastercard has introduced a new Crypto Partner Program to expand its blockchain and cryptocurrency services. This comes alongside other traditional finance integrations like NASDAQ's partnership with Kraken for tokenized stocks.

items.title: Babylon Labs Integrates with Ledger for Bitcoin Vaults
items.summary: Babylon Labs, a Bitcoin staking infrastructure developer, has partnered with Ledger to expand access to Bitcoin vaults for use as collateral in financial applications without relinquishing custody.

items.title: Crypto Markets Face 'Crypto Winter' Amid Outflows and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Crypto markets are cooling with five weeks of consecutive outflows following Bitcoin's $100K breakthrough, amid debates on Trump's pro-crypto stance versus Wall Street dynamics and rising geopolitical risks impacting prices.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Algebrik AI's Loan Origination Suite Selected by Gold Coast Federal Credit Union
items.summary: Algebrik AI announced that Gold Coast Federal Credit Union has selected Algebrik ONE, its agentic AI-powered Loan Origination Suite, to power fully digital lending and account opening experiences for members in Palm Beach and surrounding Florida counties.

items.title: Fideo Intelligence Expands Dark Web Monitoring for Fraud Detection
items.summary: Fideo Intelligence announced an expansion of its dark web monitoring and threat intelligence capabilities to help financial institutions and fintech companies detect fraud earlier and reduce payment risk.

items.title: Huawei Upgrades Banking AI and Foundation Model Solutions
items.summary: Huawei unveiled upgraded Banking AI solutions including SuperPoD offerings, AI Data Platform, and Xinghe AI Network, reducing agent development cycles from months to weeks and improving prompt accuracy by 10%.

items.title: Manulife Launches Enterprise AI Platform in Beta Testing
items.summary: Manulife announced its enterprise AI platform now in beta, providing a secure foundation for building and deploying AI agents with embedded governance and Responsible AI practices, developed in partnership with Akka.

items.title: Google Uses AI to Predict Flash Floods from Historical News Reports
items.summary: Google researchers used Gemini to analyze 5 million news articles and identify 2.6 million flood reports, creating a geo-tagged dataset called 'Groundsource' for improved flash flood prediction.

items.title: USC Undergraduates Identify Censorship Patterns in DeepSeek LLM
items.summary: A USC study found that DeepSeek retains critical internal reasoning but outputs text that 'leans toward dominant ideological or state-aligned narratives,' highlighting how large language models may omit alternative perspectives.

items.title: USC Research Uses Image Generation Models to Train Robots
items.summary: A USC study accepted to the International Conference on Robotics and Automation demonstrates using AI image generation models to train robots for complex two-handed tasks like opening jars, reducing expensive data collection.

items.title: Lawmakers Propose AI Transparency and Health Insurance Safeguards
items.summary: Minnesota lawmakers introduced multiple AI regulation measures, including requirements for companies to disclose when users interact with AI systems and prohibitions on health insurers using AI for prior authorization decisions without human review.

items.title: ITIF Report: Public Data Rules Shaping AI Development
items.summary: A new analysis examines how government regulations on publicly available data are influencing AI development, recommending focus on harmful outputs rather than restricting training data collection.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie Announces Guardian Games 2026 and Destiny 2 Updates
items.summary: Bungie revealed details in the latest TWAB including the Guardian Games 2026 launch, a new community challenge, Trials of Osiris map Javelin 4 with Corundum Hammer reward, and confirmation of a positive scenario for the June Pantheon 2.0 update amid the Destiny 2: Renegades Expansion.

items.title: Rumor: PlayStation 6 and Xbox Next-Gen (Project Helix) Launching Holiday 2027
items.summary: Rumors indicate both PlayStation 6 and Xbox's Project Helix console are targeting a holiday 2027 release; Xbox also unveiled initial tech details for Helix and a streamlined Xbox UI coming to Windows 11 PCs in April.

items.title: Pokémon Pokopia Sells Over 2.2 Million Units in First Four Days
items.summary: The new Pokémon title Pokopia has surpassed 2.2 million units sold within its first four days of release, marking strong initial sales performance.

items.title: Warhammer 40k: Darktide Introduces 'Expeditions' Game Mode
items.summary: Fatshark announced the Expeditions system, a new game mode leaving the Hive for fresh environments, with gameplay reveal scheduled for March 13; it complements adventure mode and introduces new armor.

items.title: Age of Empires Balance Patch Nerfs Champion Warriors
items.summary: The March 12 patch significantly nerfed Champion Warriors, which were dominating mid-elo ladders post-DLC, addressing exploits like early dark age scouts; criticism mounts over Microsoft's rapid post-release changes.

items.title: Dragon Ball Z: Dokkan Battle March Producer's Letter Teases Saiyan Day 2026
items.summary: Upcoming March 16 maintenance introduces Saiyan Day data download, a new story-based mode potentially tied to Saiyan or Namek sagas, and battle system updates for version 6.0.

items.title: Silent Hill 2 Remake Surpasses Five Million Players
items.summary: The Silent Hill 2 Remake has now exceeded five million players, highlighting its ongoing success.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Atlassian Lays Off 10% of Workforce to Fund AI and Enterprise Sales
items.summary: Australian software company Atlassian announced layoffs of about 1,600 employees, or 10% of its staff, on March 11 to redirect resources toward AI development and enterprise sales amid shifting market conditions.

items.title: Intel Shareholder Sues Over Alleged 10% Government Stake Deal
items.summary: An Intel shareholder filed a lawsuit against the chipmaker, claiming leadership improperly handed over a 10% stake to the US government.

items.title: Big Tech Commits Nearly $100 Billion More to Data Center Leases
items.summary: Major tech companies confirmed nearly $100 billion in additional future data center leases, pushing total commitments past $700 billion as demand for server capacity surges; Microsoft alone added 1 gigawatt of power last quarter.

items.title: Google Uses Historical News Reports to Predict Flash Floods in Data-Scarce Areas
items.summary: Google developed a system leveraging old news reports and AI to create datasets for predicting flash floods in regions lacking weather infrastructure, with potential applications to heat waves and mudslides.
